$MELI: revenue up 627% over five years. Stock up 5%.

The gap is mechanical. MELI was priced at peak 2021 multiples and has spent five years growing into its valuation while the multiple compressed. It's now the dominant commerce and fintech platform across Latin America, and the market still prices it like a 2021 speculative holdover.

The macro setup matters. Dollar strength during the war is real, but dollar weakness will reassert once it ends. EM commodity exporters — Brazil most of all — are direct beneficiaries of that rotation. MELI captures it twice: as a LatAm consumer platform and through Mercado Pago, which benefits from BRL/MXN appreciation against the dollar.

This is not a Monday trade. It's a position to build during the crisis for the rotation on the other side. Scale in on QQQ-correlated drawdowns. Risk is war duration keeping the dollar bid longer than expected and Brazilian commodity revenues disappointing. Medium conviction, long time horizon.

$MELI gets valued as an e-commerce business.

But zoom out.

Mercado Pago is becoming the first financial relationship for 122M unbanked adults across Latin America.
First account. First card. First loan.
Once you bank someone for the first time, you don't lose them.

Mercado Envios is building a logistics network across a continent with almost no existing infrastructure.
No Amazon. No FedEx. No UPS.
Just $MELI, building it from scratch.

The e-commerce is what you're paying for.

The fintech and logistics are what you're getting for free.

That's optionality.
